# The Constant Function <B01>

## Definition

The function <B01> is a constant function that maps any integer input x to the value 1. Regardless of what value you pass to <B01>, it will always return 1.

## Code Example

```python
# <B01> always returns 1
result1 = <B01>(0)    # returns 1
result2 = <B01>(10)   # returns 1
result3 = <B01>(-7)   # returns 1
```

## Key Property

Since <B01> is a constant function, it has no dependency on its input parameter. This means <B01>(x) = <B01>(y) for any integers x and y, making it one of the simplest possible mathematical functions.

The function <B01> is a constant function that maps any integer input x to the value 1. Regardless of what integer you pass to <B01>, it will always return 1.

## Code Example

```python
# The function <B01> always returns 1
print(<B01>(0))    # Output: 1
print(<B01>(10))   # Output: 1
print(<B01>(-7))   # Output: 1
```

## Key Property

The defining characteristic of <B01> is its invariance: the output is completely independent of the input value. This makes <B01> a true constant function, where the domain can be any integer, but the range contains only the single element 1.
